free_text: Study of the 10,11B + 10,11B Reactions up to E/A
approximately 5 MeV
Pelletron
SD
On. AUTHOR corrected, FACILITY updated
SD: Reaction code was changed in Subents
ERR-ANALYS moved to Subents, where it is needed.
Self-supporting 10B targets, 30 mu-g/cm2 to 50 mu-g/cm2
thick, were used. A very thin gold layer was deposited
on both sides of  the target for normalization purposes
and to minimize the target oxidation.
The scattered particles and the recoiling
nuclei were detected in coincidence by two position-
sensitive solid-state detectors.

Absolute cross sections were obtained with reference
to the elastic-scattering data.
Self-supporting 10B targets, 30 mu-g/cm2 to 50 mu-g/cm2
thick, were used. A very thin gold layer was deposited
on both sides of  the target for normalization purposes
and to minimize the target oxidation.
time-of-flight device based on a
microchannel plate (MCP) and an ionization chamber (IC)
and a position-sensitive ionization chamber(PSIC) were
used. PSIC was followed by a large area soli-state
detector. A MCP was used to detect the initial passage
of the reaction products which were then stopped in a
300  mm2 silicon surface barrier detector located at
the end of a 20 cm long ionization  chamber.
Two surface-barrier detectors, located at
-+15 degr., out of the reaction plane were used as
monitors for the relative normalizations and to follow
the carbon build up.
Ang. distrib.in mb/rad as presented on fig.10(a)
the same as DATA-ERR
